meta.name: 1 Maccabees
meta.copyright: New Revised Standard Version Bible, copyright © 1989 National Council of the Churches of Christ in the United States of America. Used by permission. All rights reserved worldwide.

text.KJV: 1:1 And it happened, after that Alexander sonne of Philip, the Macedonian, who came out of the land of Chettum, had smitten Darius king of the Persians and Medes, that hee reigned in his stead, the first ouer Greece,
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. Crisis and Response Chapter 1 - From Alexander to Antiochus. After Alexander the Macedonian, Philip’s son, who came from the land of Kittim, had defeated Darius, king of the Persians and Medes, he became king in his place, having first ruled in Greece.
text.NRSV: After Alexander son of Philip, the Macedonian, who came from the land of Kittim, had defeated King Darius of the Persians and the Medes, he succeeded him as king. (He had previously become king of Greece.)

text.KJV: 1:7 So Alexander reigned twelue yeeres, and (then) died.
text.NABRE: 1:7 Alexander had reigned twelve years when he died.
text.NRSV: Then Alexander died, after he had reigned twelve years.

text.KJV: 1:10 And there came out of them a wicked roote, Antiochus Epiphanes, sonne of Antiochus the king, who had beene an hostage at Rome, and he reigned in the hundreth and thirty and seuenth yeere of the kingdome of the Greekes.
text.NABRE: 1:10 There sprang from these a sinful offshoot, Antiochus Epiphanes, son of King Antiochus, once a hostage at Rome. He became king in the one hundred and thirty-seventh year of the kingdom of the Greeks.
text.NRSV: From them came forth a sinful root, Antiochus Epiphanes, son of King Antiochus; he had been a hostage in Rome. He began to reign in the one hundred thirty-seventh year of the kingdom of the Greeks.

text.KJV: 1:11 In those daies went there out of Israel wicked men, who perswaded many, saying, Let vs goe, and make a couenant with the heathen, that are round about vs: for since we departed from them, we haue had much sorrow.
text.NABRE: 1:11 Lawless Jews. In those days there appeared in Israel transgressors of the law who seduced many, saying: “Let us go and make a covenant with the Gentiles all around us; since we separated from them, many evils have come upon us.”
text.NRSV: In those days certain renegades came out from Israel and misled many, saying, “Let us go and make a covenant with the Gentiles around us, for since we separated from them many disasters have come upon us.”

text.KJV: 1:20 And after that Antiochus had smitten Egypt, he returned againe in the hundreth fortie and third yeere, and went vp against Israel and Ierusalem with a great multitude,
text.NABRE: 1:20 Robbery of the Temple. After Antiochus had defeated Egypt in the one hundred and forty-third year, he returned and went up against Israel and against Jerusalem with a strong force.
text.NRSV: After subduing Egypt, Antiochus returned in the one hundred forty-third year. He went up against Israel and came to Jerusalem with a strong force.

text.KJV: 1:21 And entred proudly into the sanctuarie, and tooke away the golden altar, and the candlesticke of light, and all the vessels thereof,
text.NABRE: 1:21 He insolently entered the sanctuary and took away the golden altar, the lampstand for the light with all its utensils,
text.NRSV: He arrogantly entered the sanctuary and took the golden altar, the lampstand for the light, and all its utensils.

text.KJV: 1:41 Moreouer king Antiochus wrote to his whole kingdome, that all should be one people,
text.NABRE: 1:41 Religious Persecution. Then the king wrote to his whole kingdom that all should be one people,
text.NRSV: Then the king wrote to his whole kingdom that all should be one people,

text.KJV: 1:42 And euery one should leaue his lawes: so all the heathen agreed, according to the commandement of the king.
text.NABRE: 1:42 and abandon their particular customs. All the Gentiles conformed to the command of the king,
text.NRSV: and that all should give up their particular customs.

text.KJV: 1:54 Now the fifteenth day of the moneth Casleu, in the hundreth fourtie and fift yeere, they set vp the abomination of desolation vpon the Altar, and builded idole altars throughout the cities of Iuda, on euery side:
text.NABRE: 1:54 On the fifteenth day of the month Kislev, in the year one hundred and forty-five, the king erected the desolating abomination upon the altar of burnt offerings, and in the surrounding cities of Judah they built pagan altars.
text.NRSV: Now on the fifteenth day of Chislev, in the one hundred forty-fifth year, they erected a desolating sacrilege on the altar of burnt offering.

text.KJV: 1:62 Howbeit, many in Israel were fully resolued and confirmed in themselues, not to eate any vncleane thing.
text.NABRE: 1:62 But many in Israel were determined and resolved in their hearts not to eat anything unclean;
text.NRSV: But many in Israel stood firm and were resolved in their hearts not to eat unclean food.

text.KJV: 1:63 Wherfore they chose rather to die, that they might not be defiled with meats, and that they might not profane the holy Couenant: So then they died.
text.NABRE: 1:63 they preferred to die rather than to be defiled with food or to profane the holy covenant; and they did die.
text.NRSV: They chose to die rather than to be defiled by food or to profane the holy covenant; and they did die.

text.KJV: 2:1 In those daies arose Mattathias the son of Iohn, the sonne of Simeon, a Priest of the sonnes of Ioarib, from Ierusalem, and dwelt in Modin.
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 - Mattathias and His Sons. In those days Mattathias, son of John, son of Simeon, a priest of the family of Joarib, left Jerusalem and settled in Modein.
text.NRSV: In those days Mattathias son of John son of Simeon, a priest of the family of Joarib, moved from Jerusalem and settled in Modein.

text.KJV: 2:2 And he had fiue sonnes, Ioannan called Caddis:
text.NABRE: 2:2 He had five sons: John, who was called Gaddi;
text.NRSV: He had five sons, John surnamed Gaddi,

text.KJV: 2:3 Simon, called Thassi:
text.NABRE: 2:3 Simon, who was called Thassi;
text.NRSV: Simeon surnamed Thassi,

text.KJV: 2:4 Iudas, who was called Maccabeus:
text.NABRE: 2:4 Judas, who was called Maccabeus;
text.NRSV: Judas surnamed Maccabeus,

text.KJV: 2:5 Eleazar, called Auaran, and Ionathan, whose surname was Apphus.
text.NABRE: 2:5 Eleazar, who was called Avaran; and Jonathan, who was called Apphus.
text.NRSV: Eleazar surnamed Avaran, and Jonathan surnamed Apphus.

text.KJV: 2:15 In the meane while the kings officers, such as compelled the people to reuolt, came into the city Modin to make them sacrifice.
text.NABRE: 2:15 Pagan Worship Refused and Resisted. The officers of the king in charge of enforcing the apostasy came to the city of Modein to make them sacrifice.
text.NRSV: The king’s officers who were enforcing the apostasy came to the town of Modein to make them offer sacrifice.

text.KJV: 2:19 Then Mattathias answered, and spake with a loude voice, Though all the nations that are vnder the kings dominion obey him, and fall away euery one from the religion of their fathers, and giue consent to his commandements:
text.NABRE: 2:19 But Mattathias answered in a loud voice: “Although all the Gentiles in the king’s realm obey him, so that they forsake the religion of their ancestors and consent to the king’s orders,
text.NRSV: But Mattathias answered and said in a loud voice: “Even if all the nations that live under the rule of the king obey him, and have chosen to obey his commandments, everyone of them abandoning the religion of their ancestors,

text.KJV: 2:20 Yet will I, and my sonnes, and my brethren walke in the couenant of our fathers.
text.NABRE: 2:20 yet I and my sons and my kindred will keep to the covenant of our ancestors.
text.NRSV: I and my sons and my brothers will continue to walk in the covenant of our ancestors.”

text.KJV: 2:24 Which thing when Mattathias saw, he was inflamed with zeale, and his reines trembled, neither could hee forbeare to shew his anger according to iudgement: wherefore he ranne, and slew him vpon the altar.
text.NABRE: 2:24 When Mattathias saw him, he was filled with zeal; his heart was moved and his just fury was aroused; he sprang forward and killed him upon the altar.
text.NRSV: When Mattathias saw it, he burned with zeal and his heart was stirred. He gave vent to righteous anger; he ran and killed him on the altar.

text.KJV: 2:27 And Mattathias cried throughout the citie with a loud voyce, saying, Whosoeuer is zealous of the law, and maintaineth the couenant, let him follow me.
text.NABRE: 2:27 Then Mattathias cried out in the city, “Let everyone who is zealous for the law and who stands by the covenant follow me!”
text.NRSV: Then Mattathias cried out in the town with a loud voice, saying: “Let everyone who is zealous for the law and supports the covenant come out with me!”

text.KJV: 2:49 Now when the time drew neere, that Mattathias should die, he said vnto his sonnes, Now hath pride & rebuke gotten strength, and the time of destruction, and the wrath of indignation:
text.NABRE: 2:49 Farewell of Mattathias. When the time came for Mattathias to die, he said to his sons: “Arrogance and scorn have now grown strong; it is a time of disaster and violent wrath.
text.NRSV: Now the days drew near for Mattathias to die, and he said to his sons: “Arrogance and scorn have now become strong; it is a time of ruin and furious anger.”

text.KJV: 2:66 As for Iudas Maccabeus hee hath bin mighty and strong, euen from his youth vp, let him be your captaine, and fight the battaile of the people.
text.NABRE: 2:66 And Judas Maccabeus, a mighty warrior from his youth, shall be the leader of your army and wage the war against the nations.
text.NRSV: “Judas Maccabeus has been a mighty warrior from his youth; he shall command the army for you and fight the battle against the peoples.”

text.KJV: 2:70 And he died in the hundreth fortie, and sixth yeere, and his sonnes buried him in the Sepulchre of his fathers, at Modin, and all Israel made great lamentation for him.
text.NABRE: 2:70 He died in the year one hundred and forty-six, and was buried in the tombs of his ancestors in Modein, and all Israel mourned him greatly.
text.NRSV: He died in the one hundred forty-sixth year and was buried in the tomb of his ancestors at Modein. And all Israel exhibited great mourning for him.

text.KJV: 3:1 Then his sonne Iudas, called Maccabeus, rose vp in his stead.
text.NABRE: 3:1 II. Leadership of Judas Maccabeus Chapter 3 - Judas and His Early Victories. Then his son Judas, who was called Maccabeus, took his place.
text.NRSV: Then his son Judas, who was called Maccabeus, took command in his place.

text.KJV: 3:3 So he gate his people great honor, and put on a brestplate as a giant, and girt his warlike harnesse about him, and he made battels, protecting the host with his sword.
text.NABRE: 3:3 He spread abroad the glory of his people, and put on his breastplate like a giant. He armed himself with weapons of war; he fought battles and protected the camp with his sword.
text.NRSV: He extended the glory of his people. He put on his breastplate like a giant and girded on his weapons of war and planned battles; he protected the camp with his sword.

text.KJV: 3:18 Unto whom Iudas answered: It is no hard matter for many to bee shut vp in the hands of a few; and with the God of heauen it is all one, to deliuer with a great multitude, or a small company:
text.NABRE: 3:18 But Judas said: “Many are easily hemmed in by a few; in the sight of Heaven there is no difference between deliverance by many or by few;
text.NRSV: But Judas said, “It is easy for many to be hemmed in by few, for in the sight of Heaven there is no difference between saving by many or by few.”

text.KJV: 3:19 For the victory of battell standeth not in the multitude of an hoste, but strength commeth from heauen.
text.NABRE: 3:19 for victory in war does not depend upon the size of the army, but on strength that comes from Heaven.
text.NRSV: “It is not on the size of the army that victory in battle depends, but strength comes from Heaven.”

text.KJV: 3:38 Then Lysias chose Ptoleme, the son of Dorymenes and Nicanor, & Gorgias, mighty men of the kings friends:
text.NABRE: 3:38 Preparations for Battle. Lysias chose Ptolemy, son of Dorymenes, and Nicanor and Gorgias, powerful men among the King’s Friends,
text.NRSV: Lysias chose Ptolemy son of Dorymenes, and Nicanor, and Gorgias, able men among the friends of the king,

text.KJV: 3:39 And with them hee sent fourtie thousand footmen, and seuen thousand horsemen to goe into the land of Iuda, and to destroy it as the king cōmanded.
text.NABRE: 3:39 and with them he sent forty thousand foot soldiers and seven thousand cavalry to invade and ravage the land of Judah according to the king’s orders.
text.NRSV: and sent with them forty thousand infantry and seven thousand cavalry to go into the land of Judah and destroy it, as the king had commanded.

text.KJV: 4:1 Then tooke Gorgias fiue thousand footmen, and a thousand of the best horsemen, and remooued out of the campe by night:
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 - Victory over Gorgias. Now Gorgias took five thousand infantry and a thousand picked cavalry, and this detachment set out at night
text.NRSV: Then Gorgias took five thousand infantry and one thousand picked cavalry, and this division moved out by night

text.KJV: 4:12 Then the strangers lift vp their eyes, & saw them comming ouer against them.
text.NABRE: 4:12 When the foreigners looked up and saw them marching toward them,
text.NRSV: Then the foreigners looked up and saw them coming against them.

text.KJV: 4:14 So they ioyned battell, and the heathen being discomfited, fled into the plaine.
text.NABRE: 4:14 joined the battle. They crushed the Gentiles, who fled toward the plain.
text.NRSV: So they joined battle and the Gentiles were routed and fled into the plain.

text.KJV: 4:36 Then saide Iudas and his brethren, behold our enemies are discomfited: let vs goe vp to cleanse, and dedicate the Sanctuarie.
text.NABRE: 4:36 Purification and Rededication of the Temple. Then Judas and his brothers said, “Now that our enemies have been crushed, let us go up to purify the sanctuary and rededicate it.”
text.NRSV: Then Judas and his brothers said, “See, our enemies are crushed; let us go up to cleanse the sanctuary and dedicate it.”

text.KJV: 4:37 Upon this all the host assembled themselues together, and went vp into mount Sion.
text.NABRE: 4:37 So the whole army assembled, and went up to Mount Zion.
text.NRSV: So all the army assembled and went up to Mount Zion.

text.KJV: 4:52 Now on the fiue and twentieth day of the ninth moneth, (which is called the moneth Casleu) in the hundreth fourty and eight yeere they rose vp betimes in the morning,
text.NABRE: 4:52 They rose early on the morning of the twenty-fifth day of the ninth month, that is, the month of Kislev, in the year one hundred and forty-eight,
text.NRSV: Early in the morning on the twenty-fifth day of the ninth month, which is the month of Chislev, in the one hundred forty-eighth year,

text.KJV: 4:53 And offered sacrifice according to the law vpon the new altar of burnt offerings, which they had made.
text.NABRE: 4:53 and offered sacrifice according to the law on the new altar for burnt offerings that they had made.
text.NRSV: they rose and offered sacrifice, as the law directs, on the new altar of burnt offering that they had built.

text.KJV: 4:56 And so they kept the dedication of the altar eight dayes, and offered burnt offerings with gladnesse, and sacrificed the sacrifice of deliuerance and praise.
text.NABRE: 4:56 For eight days they celebrated the dedication of the altar and joyfully offered burnt offerings and sacrifices of deliverance and praise.
text.NRSV: For eight days they celebrated the dedication of the altar, and joyfully offered burnt offerings; they offered a sacrifice of well-being and a thanksgiving offering.

text.KJV: 4:59 Moreouer Iudas and his brethren with the whole congregation of Israel ordained that the daies of the dedication of the altar, should be kept in their season from yeere to yeere by the space of eight dayes, from the fiue and twentieth day of the moneth Casleu, with mirth and gladnesse.
text.NABRE: 4:59 Then Judas and his brothers and the entire assembly of Israel decreed that every year for eight days, from the twenty-fifth day of the month Kislev, the days of the dedication of the altar should be observed with joy and gladness on the anniversary.
text.NRSV: Then Judas and his brothers and all the assembly of Israel determined that every year at that season the days of dedication of the altar should be observed with joy and gladness for eight days, beginning with the twenty-fifth day of the month of Chislev.

text.KJV: 5:1 Now when the nations round about heard that the Altar was built, & the Sanctuarie renewed as before, it displeased them very much.
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 - Victories over Hostile Neighbors. When the nations round about heard that the altar had been rebuilt and the sanctuary restored as before, they were enraged.
text.NRSV: When the Gentiles all around heard that the altar had been rebuilt and the sanctuary dedicated as it was before, they became very angry,

text.KJV: 5:2 Wherfore they thought to destroy the generation of Iacob that was among them, and thereupon they began to slay and destroy the people.
text.NABRE: 5:2 So they decided to destroy the descendants of Jacob who were among them, and they began to kill and eradicate the people.
text.NRSV: and they determined to destroy the descendants of Jacob who lived among them. So they began to kill and destroy among the people.

text.KJV: 5:17 Then said Iudas vnto Simon his brother, Choose thee out men, and goe, and deliuer thy brethren that are in Galilee, for I and Ionathan my brother, will goe into the countrey of Galaad.
text.NABRE: 5:17 Judas said to his brother Simon: “Choose men for yourself, and go, rescue your kindred in Galilee; my brother Jonathan and I will go to Gilead.”
text.NRSV: Judas said to his brother Simon, “Choose your men and go and rescue your kindred in Galilee; Jonathan my brother and I will go to Gilead.”

text.KJV: 5:68 So Iudas turned to Azotus in the land of the Philistines, and when he had pulled downe their altars, and burnt their carued images with fire, and spoiled their cities, he returned into the land of Iudea.
text.NABRE: 5:68 Judas then turned toward Azotus in the land of the foreigners. He destroyed their altars and burned the carved images of their gods; and after plundering their cities he returned to the land of Judah.
text.NRSV: Then Judas turned aside to Azotus in the land of the Philistines. He tore down their altars, and the carved images of their gods he burned with fire. After he had plundered the towns, he returned to the land of Judah.

text.KJV: 6:1 About that time king Antiochus trauailing through the high countreys, heard say that Elimais in the countrey of Persia, was a citie greatly renowned for riches, siluer, and gold,
text.NABRE: 6:1 Chapter 6 - As King Antiochus passed through the eastern provinces, he heard that in Persia there was a city, Elam, famous for its wealth in silver and gold,
text.NRSV: King Antiochus was going through the upper provinces when he heard that Elymais in Persia was a city famed for its wealth in silver and gold.

text.KJV: 6:8 Now when the king heard these words, he was astonished, and sore moued, whereupon hee laide him downe vpon his bedde, and fell sicke for griefe, because it had not befallen him, as hee looked for.
text.NABRE: 6:8 When the king heard this news, he was astonished and very much shaken. Sick with grief because his designs had failed, he took to his bed.
text.NRSV: When the king heard this news, he was astounded and badly shaken. He took to his bed and became sick from grief, because things had not turned out for him as he had planned.

text.KJV: 6:16 So king Antiochus died there in the hundreth forty and ninth yeere.
text.NABRE: 6:16 So King Antiochus died there in the one hundred and forty-ninth year.
text.NRSV: So King Antiochus died there in the one hundred forty-ninth year.

text.KJV: 6:17 Now when Lysias knew that the king was dead, he set vp Antiochus his sonne (whom he had brought vp being yong) to reigne in his stead, and his name he called Eupator.
text.NABRE: 6:17 When Lysias learned that the king was dead, he set up the king’s son Antiochus, whom he had reared as a child, to be king in his place; and he gave him the title Eupator.
text.NRSV: When Lysias learned that the king was dead, he set up the king’s son Antiochus to reign in his place. He had reared him from boyhood, and he named him Eupator.

text.KJV: 6:30 So that the number of his armie was an hundred thousand foote men, and twentie thousand horsemen, and two and thirty Elephants exercised in battell.
text.NABRE: 6:30 His army numbered a hundred thousand footsoldiers, twenty thousand cavalry, and thirty-two elephants trained for war.
text.NRSV: The number of his forces was one hundred thousand infantry, twenty thousand cavalry, and thirty-two elephants accustomed to war.

text.KJV: 6:43 ¶ Eleazar also (syrnamed) Sauaran, perceiuing that one of the beasts, armed with royall harnesse, was higher then all the rest, and supposing that the king was vpon him,
text.NABRE: 6:43 Eleazar, called Avaran, saw one of the beasts covered with royal armor and bigger than any of the others, and so he thought the king was on it.
text.NRSV: And Eleazar, called Avaran, saw that one of the animals was equipped with royal armor. It was taller than all the others, and he supposed that the king was on it.

text.KJV: 6:46 Which done, he crept vnder the Elephant, and thrust him vnder and slew him: whereupon the Elephant fell downe vpon him, and there he died.
text.NABRE: 6:46 He ran under the elephant, stabbed it and killed it. The beast fell to the ground on top of him, and he died there.
text.NRSV: He got under the elephant, stabbed it from beneath, and killed it; but it fell to the ground upon him and he died there.

text.KJV: 7:1 In the hundreth and one and fiftieth yeere, Demetrius the sonne of Seleucus departed from Rome, and came vp with a fewe men vnto a citie of the Sea coast, and reigned there.
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 - Expedition of Bacchides and Alcimus. In the one hundred and fifty-first year, Demetrius, son of Seleucus, set out from Rome, arrived with a few men at a coastal city, and began to rule there.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred fifty-first year Demetrius son of Seleucus set out from Rome, sailed with a few men to a town by the sea, and there began to reign.

text.KJV: 7:5 There came vnto him all the wicked and vngodly men of Israel, hauing Alcimus (who was desirous to be high Priest) for their captaine.
text.NABRE: 7:5 Then all the lawless men and renegades of Israel came to him. They were led by Alcimus, who desired to be high priest.
text.NRSV: Then there came to him all the lawless and ungodly men of Israel; they were led by Alcimus, who wanted to be high priest.

text.KJV: 7:26 Then the king sent Nicanor one of his honourable princes, a man that bare deadly hate vnto Israel, with commandement to destroy the people.
text.NABRE: 7:26 Defeat of Nicanor. Then the king sent Nicanor, one of his honored officers, who was a bitter enemy of Israel, with orders to destroy the people.
text.NRSV: Then the king sent Nicanor, one of his honored princes, who hated Israel and was its enemy, with orders to destroy the people.

text.KJV: 7:43 So the thirteenth day of the moneth Adar, the hostes ioyned battell, but Nicanors host was discomfited, & he himselfe was first slaine in the battell.
text.NABRE: 7:43 The armies met in battle on the thirteenth day of the month Adar. Nicanor’s army was crushed, and he himself was the first to fall in the battle.
text.NRSV: So the armies met in battle on the thirteenth day of the month of Adar. The army of Nicanor was crushed, and he himself was the first to fall in the battle.

text.KJV: 7:47 Afterwards they tooke þ spoiles, and the pray, and smote off Nicanors head, & his right hand, which he stretched out so proudly, and brought them away; and hanged them vp, towards Ierusalem.
text.NABRE: 7:47 Then the Jews collected the spoils and the plunder; they cut off Nicanor’s head and his right arm, which he had lifted up so arrogantly. These they brought and displayed in the sight of Jerusalem.
text.NRSV: Then they took the spoils and the plunder; they cut off Nicanor’s head and the right hand that he had so arrogantly stretched out, and brought them and displayed them just outside Jerusalem.

text.KJV: 8:1 Now Iudas had heard of the fame of the Romanes, that they were mighty and valiant men, and such as would louingly accept all that ioyned themselues vnto them, and make a league of amitie with all that came vnto them,
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 - Eulogy of the Romans. Judas had heard of the reputation of the Romans. They were valiant fighters and acted amiably to all who took their side. They established a friendly alliance with all who applied to them.
text.NRSV: Now Judas heard of the fame of the Romans, that they were very strong and were well-disposed toward all who made an alliance with them.

text.KJV: 8:17 In consideration of these things Iudas chose Eupolemus the sonne of Iohn, the sonne of Accas, and Iason the sonne of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to make a league of amitie and confederacie with them,
text.NABRE: 8:17 Treaty with the Romans. So Judas chose Eupolemus, son of John, son of Accos, and Jason, son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to establish friendship and alliance with them.
text.NRSV: So Judas chose Eupolemus son of John son of Accos, and Jason son of Eleazar, and sent them to Rome to establish friendship and alliance,

text.KJV: 8:18 that they would take the yoke from them, for they saw that the kingdome of the Grecians did oppresse Israel with seruitude
text.NABRE: 8:18 He did this to lift the yoke from Israel, for it was obvious that the kingdom of the Greeks was subjecting them to slavery.
text.NRSV: and to free themselves from the yoke; for they saw that the kingdom of the Greeks was completely enslaving Israel.

text.KJV: 8:22 And this is the copie of the Epistle which (the Senate) wrote backe againe, in tables of brasse: and sent to Ierusalem, that there they might haue by them a memorial of peace & confederacy.
text.NABRE: 8:22 and this is a copy of the reply they inscribed on bronze tablets and sent to Jerusalem, to remain there with the Jews as a record of peace and alliance:
text.NRSV: This is a copy of the letter that they wrote in reply, on bronze tablets, and sent to Jerusalem to remain with them there as a memorial of peace and alliance:

text.KJV: 9:1 Furthermore, when Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his hoste were slaine in battell, hee sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Iudea the second time, and with them the chiefe strength of his hoste.
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 - Death of Judas. When Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his army had fallen in battle, he again sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Judah, along with the right wing of his army.
text.NRSV: When Demetrius heard that Nicanor and his army had fallen in battle, he sent Bacchides and Alcimus into the land of Judah a second time.

text.KJV: 9:11 With that the hoste remoued out of their tents, and stood ouer against them, their horsemen being diuided into two troupes, and their slingers and archers going before the hoste, and they that marched in the foreward were all mighty men.
text.NABRE: 9:11 Then the army of Bacchides moved out of camp and took its position for combat. The cavalry were divided into two squadrons, and the slingers and the archers came on ahead of the army, and in the front line were all the best warriors. Bacchides was on the right wing.
text.NRSV: The army of Bacchides marched out from the camp and took its stand for the encounter.

text.KJV: 9:18 Iudas also was killed, and the remnant fled.
text.NABRE: 9:18 Then Judas fell, and the rest fled.
text.NRSV: Judas fell, and the rest fled.

text.KJV: 9:19 Then Ionathan and Simon tooke Iudas their brother, and buried him in the sepulchre of his fathers in Modin.
text.NABRE: 9:19 Jonathan and Simon took their brother Judas and buried him in the tomb of their ancestors at Modein.
text.NRSV: Then Jonathan and Simon took their brother Judas and buried him in the tomb of their ancestors at Modein,

text.KJV: 9:20 Moreouer they bewailed him, and all Israel made great lamentation for him, and mourned many dayes, saying;
text.NABRE: 9:20 All Israel wept for him with great lamentation. They mourned for him many days, and they said,
text.NRSV: and wept over him. And all Israel made great lamentation for him; they mourned many days.

text.KJV: 9:28 For this cause all Iudas friends came together, & said vnto Ionathan,
text.NABRE: 9:28 Then all the friends of Judas came together and said to Jonathan:
text.NRSV: Then all the friends of Judas assembled and said to Jonathan:

text.KJV: 9:31 Upon this, Ionathan tooke the gouernance vpon him at that time, and rose vp in stead of his brother Iudas.
text.NABRE: 9:31 From that moment Jonathan accepted the leadership, and took the place of Judas his brother.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan accepted the leadership at that time in place of his brother Judas.

text.KJV: 9:54 Moreouer, in the hundred, fiftie and third yere, in the second moneth, Alcimus commanded that the wall of the inner court of the Sanctuarie should be pulled downe, he pulled downe also the works of the prophets.
text.NABRE: 9:54 In the one hundred and fifty-third year, in the second month, Alcimus ordered the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary to be torn down, thus destroying the work of the prophets. But he only began to tear it down.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred fifty-third year, in the second month, Alcimus gave orders to tear down the wall of the inner court of the sanctuary. He actually began to tear down the work of the prophets!

text.KJV: 9:56 So Alcimus died at that time with great torment.
text.NABRE: 9:56 Alcimus died in great agony at that time.
text.NRSV: He died at that time with great agony.

text.KJV: 10:1 In the hundreth & sixtieth yere, Alexander the sonne of Antiochus surnamed Epiphanes, went vp and tooke Ptolemais: for the people had receiued him, by meanes whereof he reigned there.
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 - Jonathan Becomes High Priest. In the one hundred and sixtieth year, Alexander Epiphanes, son of Antiochus, came up and took Ptolemais. They accepted him as king and he began to reign there.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred sixtieth year Alexander Epiphanes, son of Antiochus, landed and occupied Ptolemais.

text.KJV: 10:18 King Alexander to his brother Ionathan, sendeth greeting:
text.NABRE: 10:18 “King Alexander sends greetings to his brother Jonathan.
text.NRSV: “King Alexander to his brother Jonathan, greetings.”

text.KJV: 10:20 Wherefore now this day we ordaine thee to bee the high priest of thy nation, and to be called the kings friend, (and there withall he sent him a purple robe and a crowne of gold) to take our part, and keepe friendship with vs.
text.NABRE: 10:20 We have therefore appointed you today to be high priest of your nation; you are to be called the King’s Friend, and you are to look after our interests and preserve friendship with us.” He also sent him a purple robe and a crown of gold.
text.NRSV: “So now we have appointed you today to be the high priest of your nation; you are to be called the king’s friend.”

text.KJV: 10:21 So in the seuenth moneth of the hundreth and sixtieth yere, at the feast of the Tabernacles, Ionathan put on the holy robe, and gathered together forces, and prouided much armour.
text.NABRE: 10:21 Jonathan put on the sacred vestments in the seventh month of the one hundred and sixtieth year at the feast of Booths, and he gathered an army and procured many weapons.
text.NRSV: So Jonathan put on the holy vestments in the seventh month of the one hundred sixtieth year, at the festival of booths.

text.KJV: 11:1 And the king of Egypt gathered together a great host like the sand that lieth vpon the Sea shore, and many ships, and went about through deceit to get Alexanders kingdome, and ioyne it to his owne.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 - Alliance of Ptolemy and Demetrius II. Then the king of Egypt gathered forces as numerous as the sands of the seashore, and many ships; and he sought by deceit to take Alexander’s kingdom and add it to his own.
text.NRSV: Then the king of Egypt gathered great forces, like the sand by the seashore, and many ships; and he tried to get possession of Alexander’s kingdom by trickery.

text.KJV: 11:13 Then Ptolomee entred into Antioch, where he set two crownes vpō his head, the crowne of Asia, and of Egypt.
text.NABRE: 11:13 Then Ptolemy entered Antioch and assumed the crown of Asia; thus he set upon his head two crowns, that of Egypt and that of Asia.
text.NRSV: Ptolemy entered Antioch and put on his head the crown of Asia. Thus he put two crowns on his head, the crown of Egypt and that of Asia.

text.KJV: 11:16 So Alexander fled into Arabia, there to be defended, but king Ptolomee was exalted.
text.NABRE: 11:16 When Alexander fled to Arabia to seek protection, King Ptolemy was triumphant.
text.NRSV: Antiochus was removed and King Ptolemy took possession of the kingdom.

text.KJV: 11:57 At that time yong Antiochus wrote vnto Ionathan, saying; I confirme thee in the high Priesthood, and appoint thee ruler ouer the foure gouernments, and to be one of the kings friends.
text.NABRE: 11:57 Then young Antiochus wrote to Jonathan: “I confirm you in the high priesthood and appoint you ruler over the four districts, and to be one of the King’s Friends.”
text.NRSV: Young Antiochus wrote to Jonathan, saying: “I confirm you in the high priesthood and set you over the four districts and appoint you to be one of the king’s friends.”

text.KJV: 12:1 Nowe when Ionathan saw that the time serued him, he chose certaine men and sent them to Rome, for to confirme and renew the friendship that they had with them.
text.NABRE: 12:1 Chapter 12 - Alliances with Rome and Sparta. When Jonathan saw that the time was right, he chose men and sent them to Rome to confirm and renew the friendship with the Romans.
text.NRSV: Now when Jonathan saw that the time was favorable for him, he chose men and sent them to Rome to confirm and renew the friendship with them.

text.KJV: 12:48 Now assoone as Ionathan entred into Ptolemais, they of Ptolemais shut the gates, and tooke him, and all them that came with him, they shewe with the sword.
text.NABRE: 12:48 Then as soon as Jonathan entered Ptolemais, the people of Ptolemais closed the gates and seized him; all who had entered with him, they killed with the sword.
text.NRSV: But as soon as Jonathan entered Ptolemais, the people of Ptolemais closed the gates and seized him, and all who had entered with him they killed with the sword.

text.KJV: 13:1 Now when Simon heard that Tryphon had gathered together a great hoste to inuade the land of Iudea, and destroy it,
text.NABRE: 13:1 IV. Leadership of Simon Chapter 13 - Simon as Leader. When Simon heard that Trypho was gathering a large army to invade and ravage the land of Judah,
text.NRSV: Simon heard that Trypho had gathered a large army to invade the land of Judah and destroy it.

text.KJV: 13:8 And they answered with a loud voice, saying, Thou shalt bee our leader in stead of Iudas and Ionathan thy brother.
text.NABRE: 13:8 They shouted in reply: “You are our leader in place of your brothers Judas and Jonathan.
text.NRSV: The people answered with a loud voice: “You are our leader in place of Judas and Jonathan your brother.”

text.KJV: 13:23 And when he came neere to Bascama, he slew Ionathan, who was buried there.
text.NABRE: 13:23 When he was approaching Baskama, he had Jonathan killed and buried him there.
text.NRSV: Trypho put Jonathan to death and buried him there.

text.KJV: 13:41 Thus the yoke of the heathen was taken away from Israel, in the hundred and seuentieth yeere.
text.NABRE: 13:41 Thus in the one hundred and seventieth year, the yoke of the Gentiles was removed from Israel,
text.NRSV: In the one hundred seventieth year the yoke of the Gentiles was removed from Israel,

text.KJV: 13:42 Then the people of Israel began to write in their instruments, and contracts, in the first yeere of Simon the high Priest, the gouernour, and leader of the Iewes.
text.NABRE: 13:42 and the people began to write in their records and contracts, “In the first year of Simon, great high priest, governor, and leader of the Jews.”
text.NRSV: and the people began to write in their documents and contracts, “In the first year of Simon the great high priest and commander and leader of the Jews.”

text.KJV: 14:4 As for the land of Iudea, that was quiet all the dayes of Simon: for he sought the good of his nation, in such wise, as that euermore his authoritie and honour pleased them well.
text.NABRE: 14:4 Praise of Simon - The land was at rest all the days of Simon, who sought the good of his nation. His rule delighted his people and his glory all his days.
text.NRSV: The land had rest all the days of Simon. He sought the good of his nation; his rule was pleasing to them, as was the honor he enjoyed, all his days.

text.KJV: 14:27 So then they wrote in tables of brasse, which they set vpon pillars in mount Sion, and this is the copie of the writing. The eighteenth day of the moneth Elul, in the hundred threescore and twelft yeere, being the third yeere of Simon the hie priest,
text.NABRE: 14:27 The following is a copy of the inscription: “On the eighteenth day of Elul, in the one hundred and seventy-second year, that is, the third year under Simon the great high priest in Asaramel,
text.NRSV: This is a copy of what they wrote on bronze tablets, which they set up on pillars on Mount Zion:

text.KJV: 14:41 Also that the Iewes & priests were wel pleased that Simon should be their gouernour, and high priest for euer vntil there should arise a faithfull prophet.
text.NABRE: 14:41 and that the Jewish people and their priests had decided the following: Simon shall be their leader and high priest forever until a trustworthy prophet arises.
text.NRSV: “The Jews and their priests have resolved that Simon should be their leader and high priest forever, until a trustworthy prophet should arise.”

text.KJV: 15:1 Moreouer Antiochus sonne of Demetrius the king, sent letters from the isles of the Sea, vnto Simon the priest, and prince of the Iewes, and to all the people.
text.NABRE: 15:1 Chapter 15 - Letter of Antiochus VII. Antiochus, son of King Demetrius, sent a letter from the islands of the sea to Simon, the priest and ethnarch of the Jews, and to all the nation,
text.NRSV: Antiochus, son of King Demetrius, sent a letter from the islands of the sea to Simon, the priest and ethnarch of the Jews, and to all the nation.

text.KJV: 15:10 In the hundred threescore and fourteenth yeere, went Antiochus into the land of his fathers, at which time all the forces came together vnto him, so that few were left with Tryphon.
text.NABRE: 15:10 In the one hundred and seventy-fourth year Antiochus invaded the land of his ancestors, and all the troops rallied to him, so that few were left with Trypho.
text.NRSV: In the one hundred seventy-fourth year Antiochus invaded the land of his ancestors.

text.KJV: 16:1 Then came vp Iohn from Gazara, and told Simon his father, what Cendebeus had done.
text.NABRE: 16:1 Chapter 16 - John then went up from Gazara and told his father Simon what Cendebeus was doing.
text.NRSV: John went up from Gazara and reported to Simon his father what Trypho was doing.

text.KJV: 16:14 Now Simon was visiting the cities that were in the countrey, and taking care for the good ordering of them, at which time hee came downe himselfe to Iericho with his sons, Mattathias and Iudas, in the hundreth threescore and seuenth yeere, in the eleuenth moneth called Sabat.
text.NABRE: 16:14 As Simon was inspecting the cities of the country and providing for their needs, he and his sons Mattathias and Judas went down to Jericho in the one hundred and seventy-seventh year, in the eleventh month (that is, the month Shebat).
text.NRSV: Simon was visiting the towns of the country and providing for their care when he went down to Jericho with his sons Mattathias and Judas.

text.KJV: 16:16 So when Simon and his sonnes had drunke largely, Ptolome and his men rose vp, and tooke their weapons, and came vpon Simon into the banketting place, and slewe him and his two sonnes, and certaine of his seruants.
text.NABRE: 16:16 Then, when Simon and his sons were drunk, Ptolemy and his men sprang up, weapons in hand, rushed upon Simon in the banquet hall, and killed him, his two sons, and some of his servants.
text.NRSV: When Simon and his sons were drunk, Ptolemy and his men rose up, took their weapons, rushed in against Simon in the banquet hall and killed him and his two sons and some of his servants.

text.KJV: 16:23 As concerning the rest of the actes of Iohn, and his wars & worthy deeds which hee did, and the building of the walles which he made, and his doings,
text.NABRE: 16:23 Now the rest of the acts of John, his wars and the brave deeds he performed, his rebuilding of the walls, and all his achievements—
text.NRSV: The rest of the acts of John and his wars and the brave deeds that he did... are written in the annals of his high priesthood.
